questions.correct_answer: Because they heard a thief in their house

questions.correct_answer: Not mentioned explicitly, but possibly that no one discovers him

questions.question: Can cleverness help us solve our problems?

notes.title: Definition of Kotwal
notes.content: Kotwal: a village official or police officer

notes.title: Importance of cleverness
notes.content: Cleverness can help us solve problems and stay safe, as seen in the story

notes.title: Effectiveness of Kotwal Rama
notes.content: Kotwal Rama was effective at his job because he quickly responded to the situation and helped catch the thief

notes.title: Characteristics of a good kotwal
notes.content: A good kotwal should be alert, responsive, and able to solve problems effectively

notes.title: Importance of community involvement
notes.content: The villagers' help in catching the thief shows the importance of community involvement in maintaining law and order

notes.title: Thief's intentions
notes.content: The thief was trying to break into a house, possibly for theft or other malicious purposes

notes.title: Villagers' role in keeping peace
notes.content: The villagers played an important role in maintaining law and order by catching the thief and reporting him to the kotwal

notes.title: Kotwal Rama's promptness
notes.content: Kotwal Rama was prompt in responding to the situation, which helped prevent further harm or damage

notes.title: Use of contractions
notes.content: Contractions like 'what's', 'I'll', and 'we're' can be used in sentences for brevity and clarity

notes.title: Verb tenses
notes.content: The story shows the use of different verb tenses, such as present simple and present continuous (e.g., 'am feeling', 'are looking')

facts.fact: A kotwal is a village official or police officer.
The story shows the importance of community involvement in maintaining law and order.
Cleverness can help us solve problems, as seen in the story.
The villagers caught the thief and reported him to the kotwal.
Kotwal Rama was effective at his job because he quickly responded to the situation.
Contractions like 'what's' and 'I'll' can be used in sentences for brevity and clarity.
The story shows the use of different verb tenses, such as present simple and present continuous.
A thief is someone who breaks into houses or buildings for theft or other malicious purposes.
The kotwal's office or courtroom is where the villagers took the thief.
The story highlights the importance of staying alert and responsive, as seen in Kotwal Rama's actions.
